gaps: No OpenAPI spec exists for the MQTT/FTPS surface.
Crowd-sniff failed to generate a spec because npm discovery returned HTTP 400 and a package tarball ended unexpectedly.
patterns: Sparse MQTT reports require reducer-based accumulation.
Exact current-job asset selection must never fall back to the newest arbitrary file.
Printer model, hardware and firmware determine field and feature availability.

novel_features.name: Calibrated completion forecast
novel_features.description: Correct the printer's finish estimate using the error pattern from prior runs of the same job.
novel_features.rationale: Requires persisted ETA snapshots and actual terminal transitions that no single printer report contains.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an automation or person needs a finish time grounded in this printer's observed history.

novel_features.name: Filament runway
novel_features.description: Estimate whether the active AMS tray can cover the current plate's remaining weight, with explicit unknown output when tray estimates or multi-material mapping are ambiguous.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining exact 3MF material demand, live progress, active-tray mapping, and AMS remaining estimates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before leaving a long print unattended or deciding which spool needs attention.

novel_features.name: Failure correlation matrix
novel_features.description: Rank the printer, filament, plate, firmware, speed, and temperature contexts most associated with failed jobs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join across job outcomes, HMS errors, printer state, AMS state, and environmental observations.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when repeated failures need evidence-backed triage rather than inspection of one job.

novel_features.name: Print-stage timeline
novel_features.description: Reconstruct one print's stages, pauses, layers, temperature recovery, and errors as an ordered timeline.
novel_features.rationale: Requires reducing many sparse MQTT observations and transitions into a chronology.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to explain where one print slowed, paused, recovered, or failed.

novel_features.name: Firmware field drift
novel_features.description: Compare first and latest persisted redacted MQTT schemas for added, removed, and type-changed fields across a selected window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires sparse-report-aware comparison across persisted redacted raw schemas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to inspect structural report changes between locally persisted observations.

novel_features.name: Repeat-job comparison
novel_features.description: Compare duration, pauses, material, errors, and outcomes across repeated runs of the same plate.
novel_features.rationale: Requires stable job identity and a local history of transitions, errors, and 3MF metadata.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to judge whether a recurring print is stable and repeatable.

narrative.headline: Agent-ready Bambu print monitoring with exact plate previews and start/finish payloads.
narrative.value_prop: For people wiring an agent or code to monitor a Bambu print and post updates to Discord, a webhook, or another automation. Use normal local access for live MQTT status, exact-current-job 3MF weight and preview, provider-neutral start/finish events, and local history; Developer Mode, LAN Only Mode, Bambu Cloud, printer control, and farm management are intentionally unsupported.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set BAMBU_SERIAL and BAMBU_ACCESS_CODE from the printer network settings. BAMBU_HOST is optional because discovery follows DHCP changes; set it only when multicast SSDP cannot cross the local network. The CLI uses normal local access only, validates the printer CA, and matches the peer certificate CN to the configured serial.

quickstart.comment: Validate local configuration and command shape without contacting or changing the printer.
Find the configured serial on the LAN without hard-coding a DHCP address.
Fetch a fresh status while keeping the agent payload compact.
Emit start and terminal lifecycle payloads with a preview attachment reference.
Record a fresh observation and current-job baseline; LAN sync does not backfill historical transitions.

troubleshoots.fix: Set BAMBU_HOST only if multicast SSDP cannot reach the printer across your VLAN or subnet.
Verify BAMBU_SERIAL against the printer; do not disable certificate verification.

troubleshoots.symptom: MQTT connects but status remains empty
troubleshoots.fix: Verify the access code and that TCP 8883 is reachable, then run bambu-pp-cli doctor --agent.
Run bambu-pp-cli doctor --agent and report the output; this CLI must negotiate implicit TLS data-channel session reuse.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or automation needs structured local truth about a Bambu print, including progress, ETA, AMS/HMS state, exact plate metadata, a model preview, and start/finish payloads. For printer control, LAN Only Mode, Developer Mode, camera walls, or farm management, use Bambuddy or another control-oriented project.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for printer control, print dispatch, movement, heating, firmware, camera control, or AMS writes.
Do not use it with Developer Mode, LAN Only Mode, Bambu Cloud, remote relays, or farm-management workflows.
Do not use it for slicing, mesh repair, MakerWorld browsing, or spool inventory management.
Do not expose the local access code in flags, logs, artifacts, chat messages, or MCP calls.

recipes.title: Automation-ready print events
recipes.explanation: Stream a start payload with ETA, material weight, and preview reference when available; enrichment failures appear in warnings. A terminal payload follows when the print ends.

recipes.title: Compact current-print summary
recipes.explanation: Keep only the fields a notification or agent needs.

recipes.title: Check filament runway
recipes.explanation: Estimate whether loaded material can finish the current plate.

recipes.title: Explain the latest print
recipes.explanation: Reconstruct stages, pauses, layer progress, temperature recovery, and errors.

recipes.title: Find recurring failure context
recipes.explanation: Group failed jobs by printer, filament, plate, firmware, speed, and temperature context.

trigger_phrases: what is my Bambu printer doing
post when this print starts and finishes
send this Bambu print to Discord
when will the Bambu print finish
